title: Frequency Maps, Counter Semantics, and Reconciliation · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for frequency maps, counter semantics, and reconciliation and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, terminal, packaging, operating-system, load, security, and release transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for inspectable frequency engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Build frequencies with explicit dictionary or Counter updates and explain missing-key and zero-count semantics.
Return a fresh result or an explicitly owned mutable result and prevent hidden cross-run state.
Assert that nonnegative frequencies sum to the accepted token count and preserve excluded-token evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Count Complexity
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Bookbot team handles case 5813 during an offline public-library reading report. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Count Complexity, keep Count Complexity connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Derive expected time and space cost from tokens and vocabulary before measuring implementation behavior.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Count Complexity, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Count Complexity precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Count Complexity, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A quiz Bookbot team handles case 4908 during a digital-humanities corpus revision. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce an owned frequency map whose nonnegative counts reconcile to accepted tokens; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive expected time and space cost from tokens and vocabulary before measuring implementation behavior.

For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Count Complexity, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Dictionary counting becomes quadratic as the number of tokens grows.
